results.title: (PDF) Certification of Lithium-ion Cells with Electrical Power Subsystem for CubeSat
results.description: The experiment required the development of a nonrechargeable battery-powered instrument for biological material life support. Crew and ship safety is vital in space flight; thus, the experiment’s use of batteries must be certified through a series of technical tests required by NASA. This article describes the certification process, including: the protection of circuits, and the electrical, vibration and vacuum tests. Finally, test results, that allowed the use of the instrument in space, are shown. ... The design process of an Electrical Power Subsystem (EPS) for a space mission is a key factor in order to be successful.
results.content:
The experiment required the development of a nonrechargeable battery-powered instrument for biological material life support. Crew and ship safety is vital in space flight; thus, the experiment’s use of batteries must be certified through a series of technical tests required by NASA. This article describes the certification process, including: the protection of circuits, and the electrical, vibration and vacuum tests. Finally, test results, that allowed the use of the instrument in space, are shown. ... The design process of an Electrical Power Subsystem (EPS) for a space mission is a key factor in order to be successful.
Phoenix consists of several subsystems and one of the most critical one is Electrical Power Subsystem (EPS), which provides, stores, distributes, and controls the satellite's electrical power. A battery pack consisting of standard cylindrical 18650 lithium-ion cells has been chosen for PHOENIX-CubeSat missions.
This fact has allowed access to the space power technologies to an increased number of users, resulting in the arrival of COTS elements to the design and development of space power subsystems (specially in nanosats). In the present work, the development of a Li-Ion battery at IDR/UPM based on COTS elements for the UNION/Lian-He mission is described.
The integrated model can be useful for engineers when developing the subsystems of their CubeSats, taking into account, for example, the battery temperature control through a heater. results.title: Guidelines on Lithium-ion Battery Use in Space Applications
results.description: This charger can · be a “smart” charger in some cases. The discharge of the cell depends on the load used but the · end voltage during discharge should not go below 2.5 V. Typical end-of-discharge voltages for · the batteries in different equipment have been 3.0 V/cell.
results.content:
This charger can · be a “smart” charger in some cases. The discharge of the cell depends on the load used but the · end voltage during discharge should not go below 2.5 V. Typical end-of-discharge voltages for · the batteries in different equipment have been 3.0 V/cell.
Specific guidance related to Li-ion systems is addressed in Attachment 1 to the Department of · the Air Force 30th Space Wing Memorandum Dated May 2005. “Joint 45 SW/SE and 30 SW/SE ... New Flight Vehicles (e.g. Ares) Battery requirements are specified in Section 3.16 “Batteries” of RCC 319-07 “Flight · Termination Systems Commonality Standard”. Batteries test and analysis requirements are specified in Sections 4.1 through 4.15 and Section ... Subsystem, and System Prelaunch Test and Launch Requirements” of RCC 319-07.
Similarly to the li-ion cells with liquid electrolyte, charging of li-ion polymer · cells requires a dedicated charger due to their unique charging characteristics. This charger can · be a “smart” charger in some cases. The discharge of the cell depends on the load used but the · end voltage during discharge should not go below 2.5 V. Typical end-of-discharge voltages for · the batteries in different equipment have been 3.0 V/cell.
Refrigerated batteries should be warmed to the optimum operational temperature suggested by · the manufacturer before discharge to obtain maximum capacity and to avoid condensation. Li- ion batteries should be stored at lower states-of-charge to avoid chemical changes in the battery
Li-ion batteries are rechargeable (secondary) batteries.

results.title: Satellite batteries - for CubeSats, nanosats, and other form factors | satsearch blog
results.description: An overview of satellite batteries and a comprehensive list of batteries for CubeSats, nanosats, and other form factors on the global market.
results.content:
An overview of satellite batteries and a comprehensive list of batteries for CubeSats, nanosats, and other form factors on the global market.
For repeated cycles of usage, as in the case of eclipsed seasons, secondary (rechargeable) batteries are preferred. Typically, nickel-cadmium (NiCd), nickel-hydrogen (NiH2), lithium polymer (LiPo), and lithium-ion (Li-ion) cells are used as secondary batteries in space applications.
The reliability of a battery depends mainly on the choice of the electrochemical system it is based on. Among the many different types, the most widely used batteries are Nickel–Cadmium (NiCad), Nickel–Hydrogen (NiH2), and Lithium-ion (Li-ion) batteries.
A classic example is the Nickel-Hydrogen batteries powering the Hubble Telescope in LEO, which were in operation from its launch in 1990 to 2009, and were then replaced by improved versions of the same type of system. Lithiumi-ion batteries are known for their dense energy, longer lifetime, and rechargeable capacity.
The highlights of Li-ion battery characteristics include a wide range of operating temperatures, and a prominent working cycle. The systems are also capable of delivering short and high energy peaks without affecting the cell.

results.title: Lithium-ion batteries for space applications in low earth orbits: Promising Technologies and testing methods - ScienceDirect
results.description: COTS 18650 cells are common in CubeSats, accepting some risk to reduce battery cost and development time. Lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) are important components of power supply systems for space applications. An important factor in the selection of LIB in this area is the requirement for reliability and fault tolerance during long-term orbital flights.
results.content:
COTS 18650 cells are common in CubeSats, accepting some risk to reduce battery cost and development time. Lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) are important components of power supply systems for space applications. An important factor in the selection of LIB in this area is the requirement for reliability and fault tolerance during long-term orbital flights.
LIBs are one of the primary space power sources due to their high energy density, reducing launch weight and cost. ... LFP cathodes are safer and longer-lasting, making them preferred over NMC for long-duration space missions. ... Cylindrical cells (e.g., 18650) are common in space due to strength, safety, and mass production economics. ... Pre-launch testing in simulated mission conditions is vital for space battery reliability, as full-life testing is impossible.
Special attention is given to the influence of low-Earth orbit conditions on the characteristics of LIB materials in CubeSat-type satellites, because this type of satellite is cheap, easy to manufacture, standardized, and can be used to develop new technological solutions in space. A separate part of the review is devoted to the input control of batteries and the prediction of LIB stability under space conditions.
Power supply systems based on lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) have become widely used in land, air, sea and rail transport. This is facilitated not only by the mobility of LIBs but also by a wide range of performance characteristics.

results.question: What causes dendrite formation in lithium-ion batteries during CubeSat missions?
results.answer: Dendrites form when batteries experience voltage, current, and temperature conditions exceeding their tolerance, potentially leading to internal shorts.

results.answer: The study identifies critical safety requirements including overcharge, overdischarge, and external short tests to maintain compliance with NASA standards.

results.question: How does EPS ensure battery safety in CubeSats?
results.answer: EPS employs protection mechanisms such as Over Voltage Protection, Over Current Protection, and Under Voltage Protection to enhance battery safety.

results.question: What are the outcomes of the vibration and vacuum tests on the battery?
results.answer: Tests indicate that there should be less than 0.1% change in Open Circuit Voltage and under 5% change in capacity post-vacuum testing.

results.question: How do charging cycles affect battery performance in CubeSats?
results.answer: Charge/discharge cycles indicate depth of discharge and state of charge, impacting battery life and mission functionality significantly.
